spec_match.rationale: This is the most spec-accurate of the AccentColor concepts: it implements the actual spec algorithm (browser picks black or white as AccentColorText whichever gives higher contrast), and the page explicitly explains the choice.

interactive.rationale: Color picker + 10 preset swatches + live patterns + 6 sim cards + a 3-row WCAG table — fully interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a contrast explorer that simulates accents, computes AccentColorText, and shows WCAG AA results. Delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Excellent on the contrast story and now includes a visible standalone display-mode probe plus non-installed simulation warning, but still lacks forced-colors coverage and difficult edge-accent presets.

references.rationale: see-also explicitly links sibling concepts; the parent feature page links the chromestatus URL. Both layers covered.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables for site chrome but the WCAG table rows include the literal strings 'Pass ✓' / 'Fail ✗' applied to classes .check/.cross that aren't defined in the local style block — falls back to inherited browser defaults rather than the design system's pass/fail colours.

openQuestions.title: Define .check and .cross styles locally
openQuestions.detail: The WCAG table applies class='check' or class='cross' to td cells but the page doesn't define those CSS rules — they're only defined on .contrast-pass / .contrast-fail. Either rename to use the existing classes or add local rules so the pass/fail colours actually show.

openQuestions.title: Cover edge accents
openQuestions.detail: Add a quick preset row for difficult accents (mid-grey #808080, near-yellow #f4ff00, near-blue #000080) where the black-or-white binary choice produces surprising results. Today's preset palette is all comfortable hues.

openQuestions.title: Show forced-colors override
openQuestions.detail: Add a 'simulate forced-colors' toggle that switches the demo into @media (forced-colors: active) mode — that's the other context where AccentColor lights up.

summary: Strongest concept in the AccentColor cluster on spec accuracy: it implements the real higher-contrast-wins algorithm and now reports whether the page is actually in standalone PWA display mode. Remaining work is unrelated to the PWA gate: undefined WCAG pass/fail classes, difficult accent presets, and forced-colors simulation.
